epigraph.quote: An amazing number of Storks flew past & there were 25 on the finials of the cathedral & the tower.

per_stop.quote: In Savernake Forest we had lunch in the shade.
We seemed to spend hours on the quai.
We saw dolphins, but poor Naomi didn't see them, or a whale.
at Amurria we stopped & walked around.
we had lunch in Burgos in the country side in shade outside someone's deserted house.
The misericords had been nailed down by a bashful bishop because they showed lewd monks.
It reminds Simon of Fawlty Towers.
There was a gap in the French column, so he sprang an attack & decimated 40,000 French soldiers in 40 minutes.
The heat caught up with us, so we didn't climb the walls.
It was one of the best swims of my life.
Outside it was 40°. Sweat poured.
just before midnight we went to a bar, to give Charles (+ us!) a bowl of gazpacho + a sort of omlette!
I swam (Simon + Charles paddled) in the enormous lake.
Someone has written a poem about it. Really lovely.
We stopped at St. Maria Eurate (again) on the pilgrimage route. There's a stone colonnade around it.
It's about the best campsite we've been in.
Charles wandered out to the loo in his Boxers. A very cross French camper shouted "Silence!"
we stopped at the pass which had beautiful views.
a nice English family - a man + woman from Poole - gave us a frozen Mars Bar.
Charles' favourite campsite. It was open, good views, a lake with ducks.
Bees were carrying an ant out of their observation hive.
Dylan (17) had had 6 friends to stay + had managed to tidy up the house well.
Thomas played is viola, Dylan played his recorder. Then Jem played his guitar.
I'm sitting in a blanket while I write this. It's so cold.
